commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Countenance. Septuagint, "the admired counsellor "(Haydock) who came into the king's presence.
Architect. 4 Kings xxiv. 14. (Calmet)
Eloquent. Literally, "mystic. "(Haydock)
Aquila and Symmachus, "enchanter."

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The captain of fifty, and the honorable man, and the counselor, and the skilled craftsman, and the eloquent orator. Tertullian of Carthage Consider whether what follows in the prophet has not received its fulfillment: “The Lord of hosts has taken away from Judah and from Jerusalem, among other things, both the prophet and the wise craftsman”; that is, his Holy Spirit, who builds the church, which is indeed the temple, and household and city of God. … And so in this manner the law and the prophets were until John, but the dews of divine grace were withdrawn from the nation.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
